stories.headline: House prosecution to call first witness on July 6 opening of Sara Duterte impeachment trial
stories.summary: The House prosecution panel said it will present its first witness on the opening day of Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial on July 6, 2026, and plans to call 57 witnesses over a requested 62 hearing days. Prosecution spokesperson Zia Adiong separately argued that Duterte's November 2024 video containing threats against President Marcos, the First Lady, and then-Speaker Romualdez was not constitutionally protected speech.
stories.context: Duterte faces four articles of impeachment: alleged misuse of P612.5 million in confidential funds, false financial disclosures, unexplained wealth, and death threats against the President and others. The defense has requested only 30 trial days.
stories.watch_for: The Senate impeachment court is expected to issue its pretrial order by June 29; the trial proper begins July 6, 2026.

stories.headline: Rodrigo Duterte's lawyers oppose ICC prosecution bid to examine items seized during his arrest
stories.summary: Defense counsel for former President Rodrigo Duterte told the International Criminal Court's Trial Chamber III on June 27 that the prosecution's request to inspect evidence from his arrest was filed too late and lacked a legal basis. The defense called the prosecution's request to access all keys held by the ICC Registry a fishing expedition and said the prosecution provided no connection between the items and the alleged crimes.
stories.context: Duterte faces crimes against humanity charges at the ICC related to alleged killings during his presidency and his earlier term as Davao City mayor. His trial is scheduled to begin November 30, 2026.

stories.headline: Former Ateneo basketball coach Tab Baldwin and 10 staff face hazing charges after two players drown
stories.summary: The Department of Justice received a hazing complaint against former Ateneo de Manila University basketball coach Tab Baldwin and 10 coaching and support staff after players Rene Baterbonia and Divine Adili drowned on June 8 during a team-building exercise in Dipaculao, Aurora. Authorities said the activity, which followed a pre-dawn run and physical games before a seawater drill at high tide, constituted hazing under Republic Act 11053. Baldwin was placed under an immigration lookout bulletin order.
stories.watch_for: The DOJ is evaluating the complaint for preliminary investigation; authorities said additional charges of homicide or reckless imprudence may follow.

stories.headline: Marcos administration proposes P7.2-trillion national budget for 2027
stories.summary: The Marcos administration submitted a proposed P7.2-trillion national budget for 2027, about P407 billion or 6 percent larger than the 2026 outlay and equivalent to 21.7 percent of estimated GDP. Acting Budget Secretary Kim Robert de Leon said the plan prioritizes completing flagship infrastructure and human capital programs, and assumes GDP growth of 5 to 6 percent, inflation of 4 to 5 percent, and an exchange rate of P60 to P62 per dollar.
stories.watch_for: The proposed budget will be submitted to Congress for deliberation.

stories.headline: Second tranche of toll increases on SLEx and STAR expressways takes effect July 1
stories.summary: The Toll Regulatory Board announced that the second tranche of toll rate increases on the South Luzon Expressway and the Southern Tagalog Arterial Road will take effect on July 1, 2026. End-to-end fares for Class 1 vehicles rise by P11 on SLEx from Alabang to Calamba and by P8 on the full STAR corridor from Santo Tomas to Batangas City, with proportionally larger increases for heavier vehicle classes.

stories.headline: Philippine fuel prices may rise by up to P1.50 per liter in the week of June 29
stories.summary: Industry analysts said Philippine pump prices could increase by as much as P1.50 per liter for gasoline and P1.20 for diesel in the week of June 29, though downward adjustments of up to P0.70 remain possible. Analysts cited firm demand from Indonesia and limited Chinese fuel exports as upward pressures on gasoline, while the easing of US sanctions on Iranian oil has provided some supply relief.
stories.watch_for: Oil companies are expected to announce final price adjustments on Monday, June 29.

stories.headline: Nearly 10,000 police officers mobilized for White Ribbon March at EDSA
stories.summary: The National Capital Region Police Office deployed about 9,602 officers on June 27 to maintain order during the White Ribbon March at the EDSA People Power Monument in Quezon City. Led by the Interreligious Leaders Council for National Transformation, the demonstration called for political reforms, accountability, and action against corruption and political dynasties. Politicians from all parties were permitted to attend but were barred from speaking during the program.

stories.headline: DepEd sustains mental health support for Tacloban school community after June 22 shooting
stories.summary: The Department of Education said it is maintaining psychosocial assistance for the community of San Jose National High School in Tacloban City, where a June 22 shooting killed three students and injured about 20 others. Support activities include counseling, storytelling, and art therapy conducted with health professionals and faith-based groups, while the two teenage suspects remain in Department of Social Welfare and Development custody.

stories.headline: Magnitude 6.5 earthquake strikes off Davao Occidental, followed by 5.2 aftershock in Sarangani
stories.summary: Phivolcs recorded a magnitude 6.5 earthquake at 7:34 p.m. on June 26 off Balut Island, Sarangani, Davao Occidental, at a depth of 45 kilometers. The agency said no major damage was expected but warned residents of possible aftershocks; a magnitude 5.2 aftershock later struck off Glan, Sarangani. Intensity V shaking was felt in parts of Sarangani and South Cotabato.
stories.watch_for: Phivolcs said it was monitoring the area for further aftershocks.

stories.headline: PAGASA warns of flash flood and landslide risks as monsoon and low-pressure area affect Philippines
stories.summary: PAGASA's 4 p.m. June 27 bulletin said a low-pressure area about 965 kilometers east of northeastern Mindanao and the southwest monsoon would bring scattered to moderate rains and thunderstorms across the country. Eastern Visayas, Northern Mindanao, Caraga, and the Davao region face the greatest risk of flash floods and landslides, while Metro Manila and western coastal areas could see isolated to scattered showers from monsoon activity.

stories.headline: Venezuela twin earthquakes kill at least 1,430 as rescuers race before 72-hour survival window closes
stories.summary: Rescuers and residents were digging through rubble on June 27 after two earthquakes of magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5 struck the coastal area of La Guaira, Venezuela, killing at least 1,430 people and leaving more than 51,000 missing. About 1,600 foreign rescue personnel had arrived in the hardest-hit areas near Caracas while damaged phone and internet infrastructure hampered efforts to locate victims.
stories.context: The UN Development Programme estimated total damages at $4.7 billion to $8.7 billion. The US repaired the runway of Simon Bolivar International Airport in Caracas to facilitate aid deliveries.

stories.headline: Hezbollah rejects US-brokered Israel-Lebanon framework deal; Israeli strikes continue in south
stories.summary: Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem on June 27 called the Israel-Lebanon framework agreement signed a day earlier humiliating and refused to link the group's disarmament to Israel's withdrawal from Lebanese territory, warning the accord could enable Israeli annexation. Israeli strikes continued in southern Lebanon after the agreement was signed, killing one person near Nabatieh al-Fawqa. Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich said Israel would remain in southern Lebanon beyond any disarmament deadline.
stories.context: The US-mediated framework agreement, signed June 26, establishes a path toward mutual recognition and normalization, including direct negotiations and a commitment to end the state of war between Israel and Lebanon.

stories.headline: Suicide bomber and gunmen attack Rangers paramilitary compound in Karachi, killing three soldiers
stories.summary: About five attackers stormed a Rangers paramilitary headquarters in Karachi's Gulistan-i-Jauhar district on June 27, with a suspected suicide bomber first ramming an explosive-laden vehicle into the compound entrance before gunmen entered the building. Three paramilitary soldiers were killed and at least two others wounded; four of the attackers were also killed. No group claimed responsibility for the assault.

stories.headline: Japan announces eight Indo-Pacific digital infrastructure projects linking Taiwan to India
stories.summary: Japan's Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications announced eight communications infrastructure projects across the Indo-Pacific, focused on undersea cables and satellite systems spanning from Taiwan to India. Officials said the initiative, framed as part of a free and open Indo-Pacific digital strategy, aims to strengthen regional economic security ties and support rising demand for AI infrastructure.
